data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-05T00:55:07.248Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me think about what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations have been problematic because the underlying Cosmos DB SDK is async-only. In earlier versions, EF Core allowed "sync-over-async" — it would block on async calls to provide sync APIs. 

In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), there was an opt-in configuration to allow sync-over-async for the Cosmos DB provider. 

The progression is that EF Core 9 started throwing by default for synchronous operations but let you opt into sync-over-async behavior, and by EF Core 11 that opt-in gets removed entirely. So synchronous LINQ methods like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, and `SaveChanges()` will no longer work against Cosmos DB because the underlying SDK is fundamentally async-only. v3 is entirely async. EF Core previously did sync-over-async which could cause deadlocks and thread pool starvation, but EF Core 9 made the Cosmos provider throw `InvalidOperationException` by default for sync calls with an opt-in to re-enable it. In .NET 11 / EF Core 11, that opt-in gets removed entirely, so all synchronous operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, and `SaveChanges()` will throw. The migration path is straightforward—convert everything to the async equivalents: `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
